tools.name: OCR
tools.description: This tool can recognize all text on the input image.

outputs.description: OCR results, include bbox in x1, y1, x2, y2 format and the recognized text.

tools.name: ImageDescription
tools.description: A useful tool that returns a brief description of the input image.

tools.name: RegionAttributeDescription
tools.description: Describe the attribute of a region of the input image.

inputs.name: bbox
inputs.description: The bbox coordinate in the format of `(x1, y1, x2, y2)`

dialogs.content: Using the provided histology figure, identify what type of cells/conditions are depicted across the panels.

dialogs.thought: Check for panel labels indicating cell types/conditions.
Orient to tissue type and what the panels show histologically.

content.content: Black-and-white histology micrographs of intestinal/colonic mucosa arranged in three horizontal panels labeled Naive, Th1, and Th2. Each panel shows colonic crypt architecture with varying inflammatory cell density and mucosal changes.

arguments.attribute: histologic features and cellularity in the Th1 panel

dialogs.thought: Verify that the Th1 panel shows increased inflammatory cellularity consistent with T-cell condition labeling.

content.content: The Th1 panel shows dense cellular infiltrates in the lamina propria around elongated crypts, with darker, more crowded nuclei compared with the Naive panel, suggesting increased inflammation/cellularity.

dialogs.content: The image shows sections of proximal colon tissue from mice colonized with pnir15.OVA-E. coli and reconstituted with different types of T cells. The sections display the effects of naive T cells, Th1 cells, and Th2 cells on the colon tissue.

whitelist: The image shows sections of proximal colon tissue from mice colonized with pnir15.OVA-E. coli and reconstituted with different types of T cells. The sections display the effects of naive T cells, Th1 cells, and Th2 cells on the colon tissue.
